title: Recommendation: Tracing depth and retry behavior for next demo run
summary: Keep claim-level trace retention but surface only short, per-claim summaries in the UI; keep detailed payloads on disk. Use a tight retry cap: one automatic retry only for transient provider errors, with explicit manifest logging. This balances reviewer usefulness and demo responsiveness while staying within the 1.15x cost ceiling.

claims.text: Candidate prompt increased median token usage by 11% but improved latency by 6% due to fewer manual reruns (not shorter single generations).

Finance will accept up to a 1.15x cost ratio for demo-quality runs if extra tokens demonstrably reduce review time; above that requires smaller batch size, stricter retry caps, or more aggressive trace summarization.
Reviewers find long traces useful only when summarized next to the failing claim; raw provider payload dumps slow review.
Demo responsiveness target: single briefings should finish under ~10 seconds; longer full runs are acceptable if progress (current case, elapsed time, artifact path) updates continuously.
Retry draft: allow one automatic retry for transient provider errors; require a manifest entry when retries affect cost/latency; reject silent repeated generations.

openQuestions: What is the current cost ratio for the demo run configuration (with claim-level traces + summaries) relative to baseline, and does it stay within the 1.15x ceiling?
How are “transient provider errors” detected/defined in the current stack (error codes vs timeouts), to ensure retries don’t mask systematic failures?
Do we need to reduce case batch size to guarantee single-briefing latency under 10 seconds during the live demo, or is UI progress feedback sufficient?
Where will the detailed traces be stored and how will artifact paths be exposed live (to satisfy rehearsal expectations)?
recommendation:
Tracing: Keep claim-level evidence retention (so evaluators can point to sources) but do not display raw provider payloads by default. Instead, show a short per-claim trace summary beside each failing claim including selected source ids, what evidence is missing, retry count, and final output path; keep full detailed traces on disk for post-demo drill-down. This matches reviewer preferences and keeps the demo dashboard readable. (S3, S6)

Retries: Adopt a strict demo retry cap: one automatic retry only for transient provider errors; no silent repeated generations. Log retry events in a manifest whenever they change cost/latency so cost is explainable in review. If costs trend above 1.15x, tighten further by reducing batch size and/or summarizing traces more aggressively rather than adding retries. (S5, S2)

Demo UX guardrails: Optimize for <10s per single briefing where possible, but rely on continuous progress indicators (current case, elapsed time, artifact path) to maintain perceived responsiveness for longer full runs. (S4)
